channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ANC supports government-led mediation in Pick n Pay retrenchment dispute impacting thousands of workers
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) Parliamentary Study Group on Employment and Labour has publicly supported Employment and Labour Minister Nomakhosazana Meth’s intervention in a major retrenchment d...

item.title: Karachi court sends suspect on two-day physical remand in wife murder case
item.description: A local court in Karachi has granted a two-day physical remand of a 63-year-old man accused of brutally killing his wife during a domestic dispute. The suspect, identified as Asghar Ali, was presented...

item.title: Two Municipal Traffic Enforcers Killed in Targeted Shooting While on Duty in Malabang, Lanao del Sur
item.description: Two municipal traffic enforcers were killed in a shooting incident in Malabang, Lanao del Sur, within the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). The victims, identified as Samar Maro...

item.title: Death of former NHL player Claude Lemieux prompts renewed attention to CTE research after family donates brain
item.description: The death of former NHL player Claude Lemieux has prompted renewed attention to chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) and the long-term impacts of repeated head trauma in contact sports. Lemieux, who...

item.title: Leading African Universities Recognised for International Collaboration and Academic Excellence
item.description: The article examines what sets certain African universities apart in the continent’s higher education landscape. It highlights four institutions that have distinguished themselves through strong inter...

item.title: Security concerns and possible protests raise tension ahead of Nairobi City Marathon
item.description: As Nairobi prepares to host the fifth edition of the Nairobi City Marathon, security agencies are raising concern over possible disruptions linked to ongoing public grievances across the country. Acco...

item.title: Duffy announces first live performance since revealing kidnapping and sexual assault experience
item.description: Welsh singer Duffy has announced that she will perform her first live concert in more than 15 years, marking a significant step in her return to public life and music. The artist, who achieved interna...

item.title: Study of 8,300 Older Adults Finds Common Table Salt-Adding Habit in Brazil
item.description: A large observational study involving more than 8,300 adults aged 60 and older in Brazil has examined how often people add extra salt to their food at the table and which factors are associated with t...

item.title: Tanzania and Russia Strengthen Economic Cooperation with New Air Routes and Investment Agreements
item.description: Tanzania and Russia have strengthened their economic and investment relations following President Samia Suluhu Hassan’s official state visit to Russia held from June 3 to 5, 2026. The visit resulted i...

item.title: Senate leadership dispute persists amid competing blocs and committee tensions in Manila
item.description: The live updates from INQUIRER.net report an ongoing leadership dispute in the Philippine Senate, where rival factions continue to assert control over key positions and committee authority. As of June...

item.title: D-Day 82nd Anniversary Commemorated in Normandy as 98 Additional British Casualties Are Added to Memorial
item.description: Veterans, military personnel and visitors gathered in Normandy to commemorate the 82nd anniversary of the D-Day landings, the Allied operation that began the liberation of Nazi-occupied France during ...

item.title: Putin dismisses Zelensky proposal for direct peace talks amid ongoing Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has rejected Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s proposal for direct face-to-face peace talks, stating that there is “no point” in meeting under current condition...

item.title: US-Iran tensions escalate with renewed strikes in Gulf amid fragile ceasefire
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran have once again escalated after both sides exchanged strikes in the Gulf region, putting an already fragile ceasefire under severe pressure. According to re...

item.title: Hamilton Leads Ferrari One-Two in Monaco Grand Prix Second Practice Session
item.description: Lewis Hamilton delivered an impressive performance during the second practice session (FP2) for the Monaco Grand Prix, setting the fastest lap time of 1:13.026 for Ferrari. The seven-time Formula One ...

item.title: Prince Harry expected to miss Peter Phillips's wedding amid longstanding family tensions
item.description: Prince Harry is reportedly expected to be absent from the wedding of his cousin Peter Phillips and Harriet Sperling in the Cotswolds. According to the article, the decision is linked not only to Harry...

item.title: US and Iran Remain Divided Over Asset Release and Nuclear Commitments as Negotiations Continue
item.description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran over a potential agreement aimed at resolving tensions surrounding Iran’s nuclear program remain active but face significant obstacles. According to rep...

item.title: Knicks Defeat Spurs 105-104 in Game 2 to Take 2-0 Lead in NBA Finals
item.description: The New York Knicks moved within two victories of an NBA championship after defeating the San Antonio Spurs 105-104 in Game 2 of the 2026 NBA Finals. The game was decided in the closing seconds when J...

item.title: Startup founders recount negative venture capital experiences and industry behavior concerns
item.description: A widely discussed thread on X has brought together startup founders sharing negative experiences with venture capitalists, revealing a range of unflattering anecdotes about fundraising interactions. ...

item.title: Super Falcons defeat Senegal 2-1 in WAFCON preparation friendly in Ikenne-Remo
item.description: Nigeria’s Super Falcons started their preparations for the upcoming Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) on a positive note as they secured a 2-1 victory over Senegal’s Teranga Lionesses in a friend...

item.title: AJK government bans JAAC under Anti-Terrorism Act and adds it to First Schedule amid planned protests
item.description: The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) government has formally declared the Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) as a proscribed organisation under the Anti-Terrorism Act, 2014. According to a ...
